F_key.description: Used for alphabetizing, cross-referencing, and creating a label when the “author” information is missing. This field SHOULD not be confused with the key that appears at the beginning of the database entry.

F_type.description: a string that describes the type of media/creative work being referenced, such as 'article', 'book', 'inproceedings', etc.

F_annote.description: It is not used by the standard bibliography styles, but may be used by others that produce an annotated bibliography.

F_authors.description: An ordered list of author(s) accredited with authorships of the creative work.

F_title.description: The title of the work (also known as the database entry) to be included in the bibliography.

F_collectionTitle.description: The title of a collection of published works, such as a book (`@BOOK`) or a periodical journal issues (`@ARTICLE`).

F_chapter.description: The chapter number of the work, if applicable.

F_crossref.description: The database key of the entry being cross-referenced.

F_journal.description: The name of the journal in which the work was published.
F_journal.$comment: FUTURE: Consider (a) creating a pattern for discriminating between string that are abbreviated and full journal names, (b) a small (self-hosted) database of journal names and their abbreviations, and (c) a subschema for journal name validation.

anyOf.description: Publication year as a 3-4 digit string, a range, or 'n.d.' (no date).
Discrete 3-4 digit integer representation or null for unmapped API data.

F_year.description: Year of Publication or, for an unpublished work (see also: '#field.altYear'), the year it was written / submitted.
F_year.$comment: Recommend type-casting to <type: string> (this applies to **all** fields), if we follow the normative reference from standard BibTeX model, but acceptable to use integer type for interoperability with external Web APIs.

anyOf.description: Allows the APA7 mandatory 'in press' (or 'In press') designator for accepted but unpublished manuscripts.

F_volume.description: The volume of the journal in which the work was published.
F_volume.$comment: Accept two primitive types with constraints. For <type: integer>, the value must be larger or equal to one (1). For <type: string>, the first character MUST be a non-zero digit, with an optional substring composed of zero or more trailing digits.

F_issueNumber.description: The issue number of the journal in which the work was published.

anyOf.description: String format that requires at least one numerical digit from 1-9 with up to four (4) optional tailing digits while also allows the option of having a second, identical numerical substring separated by en-dash '-'.

anyOf.$comment: @Technical_note: forcing the second item (`Array[1]`) to be ['U+002d']('-'), i.e.: an hyphen, so that tuple flattening (output a plain string) is cheap and efficient.

F_pages.description: The page number(s) of the work.

F_eid.description: Electronic article identifier (eid) used in place of pages for online-only journals

F_month.description: The month of publication or, for an unpublished work, the month of it was written / submitted.

anyOf.description: Discrete numeric month representation (1 = January, 12 = December).

F_address.description: Usually the address of the publisher or other type of institution.
F_address.$comment: FUTURE: Use tuple validation and create a schema (via abstraction) for address to optimize validation and data transfers across different APIs.

F_editors.$comment: This is an array of the editor(s) of the work. @FUTURE - Consider changing this to an array of author while allowing for a single string representation of the editor(s) to account for pseudonyms and mononyms of anonymous, journal-appointed editors.

F_series.description: The series to which the work belongs.

F_howpublished.description: How the work was published? / The medium where the work was published (e.g., 'Online', 'In press').
F_howpublished.$comment: FUTURE: Referencing the APA7 acceptable mediums for publications or cited works, and create a subschema (enumerated) to replace `type: string`.

F_institution.description: The institution where the work was published.

F_organization.description: The organization associated with the work, namingly theses.

F_school.description: The school where the work was published.

F_note.description: Additional notes (from the bibliographic database owner/users) about the work.

F_webIdentifier.description: A web-based identifier for the work, such as a DOI or URL.

author.description: Represents either an individual person or a corporate entity.

honorifics.$comment: Unnecessary but leave as is for now

enum.howpublished.title: Master Medium & Modality Descriptors
enum.howpublished.description: Polymorphic controlled vocabulary defining APA-7 and CFF format descriptors.

anyOf.title: Type of Contribution in '@conference' entries
anyOf.description: Controlled taxonomy defining the presentation medium or contribution modality for APA7 & CFF compliance.

anyOf.title: Clinical & Pharmaceutical Reference Descriptors
anyOf.description: Controlled format descriptors for point-of-care medical databases, drug formularies, and clinical decision-support tools (e.g., AMH, MIMS, UpToDate, eTG).

anyOf.title: Legacy & General Carrier Descriptors
anyOf.description: Catch-all carrier formats and legacy descriptors to ensure backward compatibility with older BibTeX records and rigid style guides.

doi.description: DOI (Digital Object Identifier) string format and pattern validation

url.description: URL string format and pattern validation

standardCiteKey.description: The Standard String Format of a citeKey - i.e., t-string as an algorithm.
standardCiteKey.$comment:
standard pattern = 'last name of the first author' + 'year'; note that curretly, 'authors'<type: array> == [ {'author'}, ...].

We do not have a field the capacity-(?) to retrieve only the first object of an array from the data payload for evaluation at data validation time.
